sentence.text: CR
Doctors worked on Anne's eyes to get some of her sight back.
She worked hard at school and became a star scholar.
One of the most important techniques that she learned there was the hand alphabet for deaf people.
For a person who is deaf-blind, words are shaped on the surface of the hand.
CR
CR
In 1886, the teacher in charge of the school suggested to Anne that she work with a child who had been deaf-blind since she was a baby.
This six year old girl was called Helen Keller.
She could not hear, could not see and had no language.
CR
CR
Anne said yes.
Helen was not an easy student and it was a challenge to get her to learn.
But Anne had a strong character and was determined that she would not give up on Helen.
Soon, Helen learned her first word by using the hand alphabet.
After that, Helen began to learn with speed and efficiency.
CR
CR
Later on, Anne also taught Helen how to speak.
When Helen went to college in 1900, Anne went with her to translate her books into hand language.
They worked as a team for the rest of Anne's life.
CR
CR
Helen Keller became well known.
She said that Anne was the one who made her fame possible.
Helen said that Anne was the one who deserved the praise.
Anne had found a way to teach Helen and gave her the freedom to live a whole life.
